{{Quote|Please omit unnecessary details, my dear! Recording circuits are open!|Mistress Mnemos|<ref name="The New Essential Guide to Droids">''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]''</ref>}}
'''Mistress Mnemos''' was a [[BRT supercomputer/Legends|BRT supercomputer]] hidden deep within a secret [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] stronghold on [[Fusai/Legends|Fusai]].

==Biography==
A strict and proper machine, Mistress Mnemos originally served the [[Obroa-skai/Legends|Obroa-skai]] branch library on Fusai, where she was housed in an underground chamber and plugged into a wall of storage drives over a kilometer long. When funding was cut for the library, Mnemos' data vault was maintained by local volunteers. Sometime prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] took custody of the supercomputer when they established Fusai as a safeworld.

[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] visited Mistress Mnemos to create a record of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his friends' early activities for the Alliance. However, his interactions with her were lukewarm at best, as they at least once argued over disseminating of information before 3PO could get the chance to explain the details of any of Luke's early activities.

==Behind the scenes==
Mistress Mnemos was created by [[Russ Manning]].

Two deleted strips, presumably taking place at the beginning of ''Gambler's World'', revealed that Mistress Mnemos had several security checkpoints, one of which (the final one) required the visitor (C-3PO in this case) to stand on a white line until his identity was verified, resulting in 3PO asking whether the security protocols, even for something as top secret as her, were too extreme. After arriving, he also finishes Mnemos' request to stand at the edge of the platform and divulge his activity, pointing out that something "as big and smart" as her should remember her friends more.<ref name="Deleted Strips GW">{{Blogspot|subdomain=dailysw|url=2009/09/star-wars-daily-strips-bonus-content_14.html|name=Daily Star Wars|text=Deleted strips intended for ''Gambler's World''}}</ref>
